title: swainsona swainsonioides
intro: Swainsona swainsonioides, also known as the red or yellow pea-flower, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to Western Australia and has been used traditionally by Aboriginal people for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function, and it has been used to treat conditions such as arthritis and wounds. Herbal preparations of Swainsona swainsonioides include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be made from the leaves, stems, and flowers of the plant.
